Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Horizon]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== The Captain ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon is the World Spirit of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon is a character who has horns.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon is bound to convey her crew in safety.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon refers to the narrator as Archmage.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon has seven-pointed eyes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon can change color from turquoise to bright blue.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon can initiate a digital competition for crew seats.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon can transfer a person's consciousness to a virtual space.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon is a character who has been described as having a maniac smile.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 11''' — Horizon is an entity that manages the simulation levels.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 11''' — Horizon is an entity that provides scenarios or tests.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 13''' — Horizon provided an opponent worth watching.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 13''' — Horizon is expected to bring the Combat Artist back.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 14''' — Horizon is someone who might be laughing at the situation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 14''' — Horizon is the person standing over Varic on the bridge of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 14''' — Horizon may give Raion the Captaincy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 15''' — Horizon is the entity that signed the message to Mell.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 16''' — Horizon can manifest as a holographic form.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 16''' — Horizon's systems are adaptive.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 16''' — Horizon can turn her body red.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 16''' — Horizon can manifest as a two-toned hologram of blue and red.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 16''' — Horizon is a Knight.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 16''' — Horizon is capable of moving the group within a ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 21''' — Horizon has a voice in the narrator's mind that can recall them to the ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 21''' — Horizon can manifest as multiple projections.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 22''' — Horizon is one of the few people who saw the end of the digital selection tournament.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 23''' — The narrator has a mental connection to Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 23''' — Horizon is an entity that the Captain can call upon for assistance.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon is a sleek ship resembling a diving hawk with metal plates that shine blue at the seams.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon's lights can turn bright green.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon is equipped with forward cannons that shine green and turrets that manifest out of Subspace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon's weapon systems include bright green plasma bolts, crimson lasers, and spell-enhanced bullets.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 25''' — Horizon invited qualified individuals in the galaxy to compete for her crew.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 26''' — Horizon can shift the ship's internal structure at will.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 26''' — Horizon did not tell anyone that the narrator is his Captain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 27''' — Horizon is a character who can conjure floating eyeballs to act as scanners.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 28''' — Horizon is a spirit that manifests a humanoid avatar.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 29''' — Horizon is the ship's spirit and can project a body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 35''' — Horizon is a projection on The Last Horizon with hands folded and horns pointed to the ceiling.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 35''' — Horizon is a projection that can repair itself but lacks specific materials.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 38''' — Horizon has monitors around the walls of its room.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 39''' — Horizon's systems can only be waken by the greatest.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 39''' — Horizon is a being that can re-form from lines of blue, red, and green light.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 40''' — Horizon can project holographic words onto the air.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 41''' — Horizon refers to Raion as her Knight.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 41''' — Horizon is the ship's spirit and the World Spirit of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 42''' — Horizon is a character who provides combat support and uses cannons.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 44''' — Horizon possesses Aethertech scanners.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 44''' — Horizon can manifest delicate humanoid hands made of blue light.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 44''' — Horizon can manifest guns from walls.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — Horizon is a vessel of heroes and the last horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — Horizon can control thousands of hands and eyes through her magic.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — Horizon's magic consists of spells that are memories of past lives brought to life.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — Horizon calls herself a World Spirit.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 45''' — Horizon's magic is a pinnacle spell.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 46''' — The narrator has been battling to control Horizon's magic.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 47''' — Horizon exists in a four-colored construct of a body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 48''' — Horizon's world has a sky that resembles stars.}}

== The Engineer ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 2''' — Horizon is an entity capable of adjusting the ship's structure and projecting holograms.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 7''' — Horizon refers to the ship The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon is the entity or AI responsible for holding The Last Horizon together.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon is a spirit that relays orders and responds to the Captain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 10''' — Horizon is a spirit.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 16''' — Horizon is a person on the bridge of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 17''' — Horizon is a product of magic and fabricators working in concert.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 17''' — Horizon intends to assemble a crew to become complete.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 18''' — Horizon moved the crew to the ritual room at the heart of the ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 18''' — Horizon is part of the crew/group associated with The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 19''' — Horizon's Aethertech gift empowers the narrator's drones, fabricators, and self-repair function.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 21''' — Varic believed they could win with Horizon's help.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 22''' — The Horizon allows any crew member to enter or leave Omega's room.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 22''' — Horizon can cooperate with Omega to tap into the Subline.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 23''' — Horizon is a projection capable of being projected in six colors.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 23''' — Horizon can add substance to herself using Aether.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon can print Nova-Bots from carbon, silicon, iron, and copper by stretching her Engineer's gift.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 25''' — Horizon is a being capable of materializing within the Terminus Mundi spell.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 25''' — Horizon believes that three activations at once would be cause for worry.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 26''' — Horizon is capable of smiling very widely.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 27''' — Horizon is an entity associated with contract magic.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 28''' — Horizon is a construct that was summoned at the Magic Tower.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 29''' — Horizon is a Zenith Device.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 29''' — Horizon is the sister of an unnamed brother.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 29''' — Horizon is a being made of magic and light.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 29''' — Horizon has brothers and sisters.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 30''' — Horizon is a World Spirit that can flicker and reappear.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 32''' — Horizon is a being that can weave herself from six colors of light.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 33''' — Horizon is the Captain of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 34''' — Horizon can teleport the narrator to safety.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 36''' — Horizon is attempting to restore contact with Starhammer.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 36''' — Horizon does not have millions of individual eyes and hands memorized.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 37''' — Fabrication at Horizon takes hours.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 38''' — Horizon is a ship with firepower beyond its size class.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 38''' — Horizon is tasked with firing on Starhammer's position.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 38''' — Horizon communicates with the narrator via an audio implant.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 39''' — Horizon is a character who protested being blamed for not delaying Raion's teleportation.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 41''' — Horizon is an android who claims to be at one hundred percent function.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 44''' — Horizon has teleportation abilities.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 45''' — Horizon keeps the narrator linked to others.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 47''' — Horizon can manifest thousands of hands outside the ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 47''' — Horizon is a wizard captain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 47''' — Horizon has Aether-dependent systems and shielding that render her invincible.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 48''' — Horizon has a cannon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 48''' — Horizon is capable of generating a red shield.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 48''' — Horizon will go dormant if the Captain dies.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 51''' — Horizon is needed to find the narrator.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 53''' — Horizon is the sentient entity/consciousness of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 53''' — Horizon has gnarled horns and a six-colored body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 54''' — Horizon is likely involved with Shadow Ark.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 55''' — Horizon possesses a treasure vault.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 55''' — Horizon is the sister of Shadow Ark.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 55''' — Horizon claims she would rather be scuttled and burned than share her Captain with Shadow Ark.}}

== The Knight ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 3''' — Horizon is a crew that the narrator belongs to.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 4''' — Horizon provides briefings and manages teleportation systems.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 4''' — Horizon is an entity the narrator can communicate with.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 4''' — The narrator signals Horizon to take them back up.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 5''' — The narrator contacted Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 6''' — Horizon is a concept or entity that can hear Raion's wishes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon's appearance is that of a woman in a long, flowing dress with gnarled horns resembling tree roots and seven-pointed stars in her eyes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon is drawn in six colors of light.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon is a member of the team.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 11''' — Horizon is the World Spirit of the Zenith Starship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 13''' — Horizon is a ship that can be kept in teleporter range.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 14''' — Horizon is a voice that comes from the walls.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 17''' — Horizon may need to be moved to confront the D’Niss.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 22''' — Horizon is an entity with immaterial horns that can sink into a person's skull.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 22''' — Horizon dropped to her knees and wailed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon is a hero of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon can project her body into lessons.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 28''' — Horizon is a statue in Varic's room.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 28''' — Horizon can manifest an eye and increase light in a room.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 28''' — Horizon is a character who can detect chemical or biological attacks.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 28''' — Horizon manifested in front of the narrator.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 32''' — There is a life-size statue of Horizon in the narrator's medical room.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 33''' — Horizon can keep watch over her own hallways.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 35''' — Horizon has previously driven Hesh’ellik away from the galaxy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 35''' — Horizon can weaken incoming magical scans.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 36''' — Horizon is a crew member who can use shields to block sound.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 37''' — Horizon is a ship that can be commanded to destroy enemies.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 38''' — Horizon is a member of the crew who provides information and can transport people.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 39''' — Horizon is protected by the greatest magical wards of the Zenith Era.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 40''' — Horizon is a Zenith Starship that can share the burden of spellcasting.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 40''' — Horizon is a character that can flicker into holographic existence.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 40''' — Horizon can pilot the ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 41''' — Horizon is a character who can be called via communication.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 41''' — Horizon is a crew member who communicates with the Captain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 42''' — Horizon is a crew member of The Last Horizon who joined the call uninvited.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 43''' — Horizon claims the bridge.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 44''' — Horizon is capable of transporting the pilot out of the Divine Titan with their assistance.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 45''' — Benri has a connection to Horizon.}}

== The Pilot ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 3''' — Horizon is a holographic entity with gnarled tree-branch horns and seven-pointed stars in her eyes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 4''' — Horizon can transport the narrator away.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 4''' — The Captain signaled their processor to call Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 7''' — Horizon perceived the narrator as seven people during their first meeting.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon collected gemstones from nearby planets.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon is an entity that communicates through the ship's walls.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon is the entity that controls The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 8''' — Horizon found a trophy of the Iron King's defeat.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon is a holographic form that can swirl into the center of a cabin.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon has control over her internal layout.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 9''' — Horizon is the name of the ship (The Last Horizon) that can teleport the Captain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 10''' — Horizon is a character with comprehensive sensors, magical eyes, and an Engineer's drones.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 10''' — Horizon's advanced scanners revealed that the Grand Hive is hollow.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 14''' — Horizon jumped in to save the ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 14''' — Horizon can manifest the narrator's staff in a flash of blue light.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 14''' — Horizon is unaffected by the Temporal Field effect.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 14''' — Horizon is addressed by the Zenith Processor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 15''' — Horizon is an entity capable of obeying orders to remove someone from a channel.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 16''' — Omega may be imprisoned within Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 16''' — Horizon is a member of The Last Horizon's crew who burns at the front of the bridge.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 18''' — Horizon is reported to be gone.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 20''' — Horizon is a crew member who can train plasma cannons.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 23''' — Horizon's adopted form is a woman with gnarled horns.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 23''' — Horizon's inner form is a seven-pointed star.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 23''' — Horizon's earliest memories are of being a vessel of heroes who spread light in the galaxy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 23''' — Horizon believes the task requires heroes pooling resources to save the galaxy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon has twisted-root horns.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon can pilot the ship from anywhere.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon is the Captain's starship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 24''' — Horizon monitors incoming ships via sensors.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 25''' — Horizon transmits video of her face to the Captain's wrist console.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 26''' — Horizon manifested in front of the mages sketched in six colors.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 28''' — Horizon was unable to send the staff directly to the narrator.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 28''' — Horizon used magic to teleport.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 29''' — Horizon is a presence that can flicker and appear beside Omega.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Horizon's crew needs to talk.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Horizon leans on Omega's shoulders.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 31''' — Horizon has walls engraved with runes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 31''' — Horizon has a floor plan that can be mapped.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 31''' — Horizon can conjure hands.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 31''' — Horizon is a character who refers to themselves as Captain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 35''' — Horizon is a character who can drift and assist the Captain with data.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 35''' — Horizon can manifest several dozen eyes around her.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 35''' — Horizon can see more than any starship in history when her Pilot and Engineer contribute their powers to her.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 36''' — Horizon is a commander who can manifest herself.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 37''' — Horizon painted over Sola's armor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 37''' — The narrator signaled Horizon to withdraw.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 37''' — Horizon used a spell to transport the narrator and another person away.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 40''' — Horizon is the Pilot's companion and can extend herself to almost ten feet tall.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 40''' — Horizon can teleport anyone to a planet from anywhere in the system.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 40''' — Horizon can grow a table and chairs out of the deck.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 42''' — Horizon's usual craft are more stylish than the shuttle used.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 43''' — Horizon is a character mentioned by Prism regarding their taste.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 46''' — Horizon's security is difficult to bypass.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 46''' — Horizon is currently distracted.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 47''' — Horizon communicates with Omega through his processor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 47''' — Horizon refers to Omega as 'My Pilot'.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 47''' — Horizon is a character who can stream video into an optical implant.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 48''' — Horizon has a voice that can cut through restrictions and speak into the Captain's Processor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 48''' — Horizon's Pilot is dead.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 48''' — Horizon can project herself in miniature.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 48''' — The narrator cannot swear on behalf of Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 49''' — Horizon is a pilot for The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 49''' — The narrator attempts to contact Horizon.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
